expectations.text: Distinguishes capital expenses (equipment) from revenue expenses (supplies, website costs)
expectations.evidence: Lines 15-29 clearly separate revenue expenses (supplies, website) from capital expenditure (equipment with lasting value), explaining AIA treatment and cash basis applicability.

expectations.text: Mentions capital allowances (e.g. Annual Investment Allowance) for equipment
expectations.evidence: Lines 27-29 explicitly name 'Annual Investment Allowance (AIA)' providing '100% relief in the year of purchase' and explain cash basis simplification.

expectations.text: Explains sideways loss relief to offset loss against employment income
expectations.evidence: Lines 47-67 provide detailed explanation of sideways loss relief with clear worked example: £42,000 salary less £6,600 loss = £35,400 taxable income, yielding £1,320 tax saving.

expectations.text: Mentions early years relief as an option for a new business
expectations.evidence: Lines 71-82 explicitly discuss 'Early Years Loss Relief' allowing carry-back against 'three tax years before the loss year' (2023/24, 2022/23, 2021/22).

expectations.text: Addresses WFH costs (flat rate or actual proportional costs for spare bedroom)
expectations.evidence: Lines 128-145 provide both methods: flat rate option (£10-26/month based on hours worked) and actual cost proportion method with detailed guidance.
Lines 149-165 explicitly state 'you must register for Self Assessment and file a return' with deadlines (5 October 2025, 31 January 2026) and registration process details.

expectations.text: Warns about hobby vs trade test — business must be commercial to claim relief
expectations.evidence: Lines 89-100 explain HMRC hobby vs trade test, listing assessment criteria (expectation of profit, businesslike operation, time dedication, growth steps) and advising record-keeping.

expectations.text: Provides a realistic estimate of potential tax saving from the loss
expectations.evidence: Line 65 calculates: £6,600 loss × 20% basic rate = £1,320 tax saving. Summarized again at line 178.
